repositories.name: github/awesome-copilot
repositories.description: Official GitHub-maintained curated list of Copilot customization resources. Includes meta-instructions for writing agents, skills, instructions, and prompts. Contains shared skills (prompt-builder, github-copilot-starter, make-skill-template). Learning hub with articles.
whatToUse: Meta-instructions (.github/instructions/) as templates for writing your own customization files
Shared skills (.github/skills/) importable into your workspace
Learning hub articles for onboarding new team members
Example patterns to extract durable principles (not to copy verbatim)
whatToAvoid: Treating any single example as the platform standard
Copying full file contents without understanding and adapting
Using outdated examples without checking against current VS Code docs

repositories.name: anthropics/skills
repositories.description: Cross-vendor skill collection compatible with both VS Code Copilot and Claude. Demonstrates the Agent Skills open standard (agentskills.io) in practice.
whatToUse: Folder structure patterns for packaging your own skills
whatToAvoid: Assuming all skills work identically in VS Code and Claude — tool names differ

repositories.name: agentskills.io
repositories.description: Open standard for agent skills defining SKILL.md format, folder structure, discovery patterns, and progressive loading contract. Supported by VS Code, Claude, and other tools.
whatToUse: Standard format for creating portable skills
whatToAvoid: Implementing non-standard skill formats that only work in one tool

officialDocumentation.title: VS Code Copilot Customization
officialDocumentation.covers: All six primitives: instructions, agents, skills, prompts, hooks, subagents
officialDocumentation.freshness: Updated with each VS Code release

officialDocumentation.title: GitHub Copilot Documentation
officialDocumentation.covers: GitHub.com surfaces: repository instructions, coding agent, organization policies

officialDocumentation.title: VS Code Release Notes
officialDocumentation.covers: Feature additions, deprecations, behavior changes per release

vsCodeCommands.description: Opens the Chat Customizations editor showing all loaded instructions, agents, skills, prompts, and hooks in one place
AI-assisted agent creation with clarifying questions and correct frontmatter generation
AI-assisted skill creation with SKILL.md scaffolding

importingCommunityResources: 1. Identify a shared skill or instruction from awesome-copilot or anthropics/skills
2. Read the source to understand what it does — never import blindly
3. Copy the skill folder into .github/skills/ (or .claude/skills/ for Claude compat)
4. Verify frontmatter fields match your tool version (VS Code, Claude, etc.)
5. Test with the Chat Customizations editor to confirm the skill loads correctly
6. Adapt the content to your project's conventions and terminology
contributingBack: 1. Extract generalizable conclusions from your audit (strip all project-specific context)
2. Format as a conclusion packet matching the community cache schema
3. Submit via the community-cache-submit flow (opt-in only)
4. Consolidation pipeline validates, scores, and promotes high-quality contributions
stayingCurrent: 1. Check VS Code release notes monthly for Copilot customization changes
2. Watch awesome-copilot for new shared skills and instructions
3. Run audits periodically — the research phase will detect deprecated patterns
4. Monitor the deprecations pack in the community cache for known field/feature changes
